message:
fix(connectors): extend InvalidInput error variant migration to remaining ~34 connectors

Continue the error taxonomy cleanup begun in 67c0c658, finishing the
sweep across the remaining connectors. Two sub-patterns appear in
this wave:

Pattern A — rewire existing InvalidInput to InvalidRequest{code:1005}
  (error.rs-only, ~3 lines each). These connectors already had the
  InvalidInput(String) variant but were mapping it to
  FcpError::Internal; correct the mapping so validation errors
  surface as FcpError::InvalidRequest{code:1005,..} instead of
  being misclassified as internal server errors.

  Connectors: algolia, amplitude, clickup, discord, docusign,
  elasticsearch, kubernetes, mixpanel, pandadoc, posthog,
  salesforce, snowflake, trello, twitter.

Pattern B — add InvalidInput(String) + migrate call sites
  (error.rs + connector.rs, sometimes client.rs). Full introduction
  of the variant plus rewrites of Api{status_code:400,..} call
  sites to Error::InvalidInput(..), plus test matchers updated
  to expect the new variant.

  Connectors: annas-archive, asana, bigquery, box, evernote,
  gitlab, grafana, homeassistant, logseq, mailchimp, monday,
  pulumi, roam, segment, semanticscholar, sendgrid, sentry,
  spotify, terraform, zapier.

Connector-only migration (variant already existed from an earlier
commit; this wave migrates additional call sites and client.rs
helpers): asana, bigquery, box, duckdb, monday, pulumi, segment,
semanticscholar.

Total scope: ~34 connectors across 68 files. Purely mechanical
taxonomy migration — no behavioral change for successful paths,
only more accurate error classification for input-validation
failures. Matches the approach and style of 67c0c658.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
